subject: Sociology
title: Sweatshops and Global Labor
description: Learn Sweatshops and Global Labor in one minute.

hook.text: Your $20 shirt was sewn by a woman in Bangladesh earning $3 a day. Global supply chains connect comfort to suffering.

buildup.text: Corporations move production to countries with cheap labor and weak regulations to maximize profit.

discovery.text: The Rana Plaza factory collapse in 2013 killed 1,134 workers. Safety warnings had been ignored by owners and brands.

twist.text: Some economists argue sweatshops are still better than local alternatives. Workers often disagree when asked directly.

climax.text: The real issue isn't whether these jobs exist, but who profits most and who bears all the risk.

punchline.text: Cheap goods have a price. Someone else is paying it.

quiz.question: What does the Rana Plaza disaster reveal about global labor?
options: Worker safety is sacrificed for corporate profit
Global supply chains benefit workers equally
